PARLIAMENT QUESTION: SOLID WASTE MANAGEMENT AT MOUNTAIN TOURIST SITES
articles.full_content:
The Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026, provide the statutory framework for environmentally sound management of solid waste in the country by respective State Governments / Urban & Rural local bodies / Bulk Waste Generators, as defined under the Rules. The rules inter-alia mandate four-stream segregation of solid waste at source into wet waste, dry waste, sanitary waste and special care waste and prescribe clear definition of bulk waste generators who have a mandate to fulfil Extended Bulk Waste Generator Responsibility, in order to ensure that the waste generated is collected, transported and processed. The rules provide for the online tracking and monitoring of all stages of solid waste management through Centralised Online Portal including collection, transportation, processing and disposal. The rules have special provision for solid waste management in hilly areas and islands. The rules prescribe key role and responsibilities of State/UT Governments as well as Urban and Rural Local Bodies and Bulk Waste Generators in the implementation of the Rules.
While State Governments are primarily responsible for sanitation, Swachh Bharat Mission Urban 2.0 (SBM U 2.0) and Swachh Bharat Mission (Gramin) [SBM (G)] provides central assistance to States / UTs for solid waste management in urban and rural areas of the country. Under Swachh Bharat Mission funds are allocated to Northeast States and Himalayan region States with fund sharing pattern of 90:10 between Centre and State, for solid waste management as per scheme Guidelines. The State-wise funds allocated to Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Jammu and Kashmir, Sikkim and Northeastern hill States under SBM-U 2.0 and SBM (G) is given below:

Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB), based upon the information provided by State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs), compiles and publishes the annual report on implementation of Solid Waste Management Rules, which includes the details of solid waste generation, collection and treatment of solid waste in all states including Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Jammu and Kashmir, Sikkim and Northeastern hill States. The annual reports are available on CPCB website: https://cpcb.gov.in/status-of-implementation-of-solid-wasterules/
This information was provided by MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE, SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH, in a written reply to a question in Rajya Sabha today.
(Rajya Sabha US Q548)

articles.title: PARLIAMENT QUESTION: IMPACT OF NATIONAL CLEAN AIR PROGRAMME ON PM2.5 LEVELS
articles.full_content:
Pollution levels across countries are not directly comparable due to differences in geographical settings, weather conditions, and national circumstances. Air pollution is the collective result of multiple anthropogenic factors vehicular and industrial emissions, dust from construction & demolition activities, roads and open areas, burning of biomass and municipal solid waste, landfill fires and other dispersed sources compounded by adverse meteorological conditions.
Government of India has notified the National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS) for 12 air pollutants including PM10 and PM2.5 to safeguard public health and environment quality. To provide air quality information and associated health implications in an easily understandable manner, Air Quality Index (AQI) was launched by the Government in 2015.
To address air pollution in urban areas in Mission mode, National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019 by the Government in 130 Cities across the country which includes Pune, Surat, Hyderabad, Mumbai and Delhi. It is a multi-sectoral initiative involving the coordinated efforts of the Central and State Governments, Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) and other stakeholders. NCAP emphasizes source specific mitigation measures through city, state, and national-level clean air action plans and it is monitored by various committee constituted at Central, State and District level. State level Clean Air Action Plans prepared by States/UTs address both PM10 and PM2.5 through coordinated approach by implementing various policy and enforcement measures.
Air quality performance based incentive grant of ₹ 16423.56 crore has been released to targeted cities under National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) as a critical gap funding to implement air pollution mitigation measures, during FY 2019-20 till 2025-26.
To address air pollution in Delhi-NCR, Government has established the Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) under the Commission for Air Quality Management in NCR and Adjoining Areas Act, 2021 for better coordination, research, identification and resolution of problems of air pollution in Delhi- NCR and adjoining areas. CAQM has been provided powers under the Act to take measures and issue directions to the various agencies in the National Capital Region for protecting and improving air quality.
The focused and coordinated actions by 130 cities under NCAP have shown positive results with 108 cities recorded reductions in annual PM10 concentrations during 2025-26, compared to 2017-18, with 72 cities achieving reductions of more than 20% and 26 cities achieving reductions of more than 40%. Further, 14 cities met the National Ambient Air Quality Standards in FY 2025-26 (Standards ≤ 60 µg/m3), compared to six cities in 2017-18.
Further, out of the 130 cities under NCAP, 65 cities have met the National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS) for PM2.5 levels (Standard ≤ 40 µg/m³) in 2025-26. The PM2.5 level in Pune, Hyderabad, Surat, Delhi and Mumbai

Unraveling global aerosol diversity provides the key to decoding Climate Change
articles.full_content:
A recent study that identified regional variations in the distribution of seven types of aerosols across various parts of the world and their associated radiative effects found that South Asia is largely affected by dust mixed with pollution which produces the highest Aerosol Radiative Forcing (ARF) and atmospheric heating rates.
The study offers a global, long-term perspective of aerosols that can help refine climate models and improve predictions.
Aerosols, tiny particles suspended in the atmosphere, play a crucial yet complex role in shaping our planet's climate. From desert dust storms to urban emissions and forest fires, these microscopic particles scatter, absorb, and interact with sunlight in different ways, altering the planet’s energy balance and affecting weather, clouds, and rainfall.
Researchers from the Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA), Bengaluru, India, and Aryabhatta Research Institute of Observational Sciences (ARIES), Nainital, both autonomous research institutes of the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India, and other institutes from India and abroad studied the classification of global aerosol types and radiative effects.
This study, published in the Atmospheric Environment Journal , was based on Particle Linear Depolarization Ratio (PLDR) and Single Scattering Albedo (SSA) from the AERosol Robotic NETwork (AERONET). Seven main aerosol types were identified, and these are pure dust (PD), dust-dominated mixtures (DDM), pollution-dominated mixtures (PDM), very weakly absorbing (VWA), weakly absorbing (WA), moderately absorbing (MA), and strongly absorbing (SA). While pure dust (PD) aerosols dominate in northern Africa, a mixture of dust and pollution or dust-dominated mixtures (DDM) are common in South Asia. Urban and industrial regions in Europe and North America are characterized by less-absorbing aerosols, VWA, WA, and MA aerosols. Tropical regions, prone to biomass burning, are dominated by strongly absorbing (SA) aerosols. Among the aerosol types, SA contributes the highest atmospheric forcing (30.14 ± 8.04 Wm −2 ) and heating rate (0.85 K Day −1 ), while VWA has the lowest forcing (7.83 ± 4.12 Wm −2 ) and heating rate (0.22 K Day −1 ).
Fig 1: Global map of 171 AERONET sites shown with maximum frequency of occurrence (%) among the seven aerosol types, namely, PD, SA, PDM, MA, DDM, WA, and VWA in the current work.
Strongly absorbing (SA) aerosols produce the highest atmospheric forcing (30.14 ± 8.04 W m⁻²), whereas very weakly absorbing aerosols produce the lowest (7.83 ± 4.12 W m⁻²).
The study analyzed data from 171 ground-based monitoring stations, or AERONET sites, across six continents, spanning over 30 years. The researchers classified aerosols into seven specific types and studied their radiative forcing using advanced methods. The classification improved on earlier studies, which typically grouped aerosols into fewer, broader categories .

The study offers a global, long-term perspective of aerosols that can help refine climate models and improve predictions. The implementation of PM Vishwakarma has commenced in the State of West Bengal following the issuance of a notification by the Government of West Bengal constituting the State Monitoring Committee and District Implementation Committees on 14.05.2026 for the roll-out of the Scheme in the State.
Under Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY), a total of 5.82 crore loans amounting to Rs. 3.55 lakh crore have been disbursed since inception i.e. 08.04.2015 till 26.06.2026 in the State of West Bengal.

• Public Procurement Policy for MSEs: Central Ministries and CPSEs are mandated to procure atleast 3% of their annual procurement from women owned Micro and Small Enterprises, thereby facilitating market access.
• Credit Guarantee Scheme for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE): women owned Micro and Small Enterprises are eligible for enhanced guarantee coverage of upto 90% (as against 75% for others) and a 10% concession in the annual guarantee fee.
• PMEGP: Women beneficiaries are categorized under Special Category and eligible for a higher Margin Money (MM) Subsidy rate of upto 35% and lower own contribution of 5% of project cost, compared to 25% MM Subsidy rate and 10% own contribution of project cost for beneficiaries under General category.

• Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme: women entrepreneurs are provided financial assistance of upto 100% of the admissible expenditure for participation in domestic trade fairs, compared to 80% for other entrepreneurs.

articles.title: Ministry of MSME implements the Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGS) for Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) to provide credit guarantees for the credit facilities extended to MSEs
articles.full_content:
The Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprise (MSME) implements Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGS) for Micro Small Enterprises (MSEs) through Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) to provide credit guarantees for the credit facilities extended to MSEs, including first-generation entrepreneurs, women-led enterprises, and units located in backward districts, without collateral security or third-party guarantees, across the Country .
Measures taken by the Government to improve credit flow and enhance awareness of the CGTMSE scheme among MSEs include:
• The guarantee ceiling for the loan extended under CGS was enhanced from Rs. 5 crore to Rs. 10 crore w.e.f 01.04.2025.
• Annual Guarantee Fees (AGF) structure revised with 50% reduction in standard rate to as low as 0.37% per annum.
• Enhanced extent of guarantee for MSEs led by special category including women led MSEs.
• As per Reserve Bank of India’s Master Direction - Lending to Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) Sector dated 09.02.2026, all Scheduled Commercial Banks are mandated not to accept collateral security in the case of loans up to Rs.20 lakh extended to units in the MSE sector.
• To promote credit flow to MSEs in Identified Credit Deficient Districts (ICDD) as per RBI, CGTMSE offers 10% discount on the AGF and extend an additional 5% guarantee coverage.
• To provide enhanced guarantee coverage for credit facilities extended by Member Lending Institutions (MLIs) to the MSEs engaged in Manufacturing Sector situated in the State of Tamil Nadu, a special guarantee scheme “Tamil Nadu Credit Guarantee Scheme (TNCGS)” in collaboration with Government of Tamil Nadu is being implemented.
• The performance of banks under the CGTMSE Scheme and the overall flow of credit to the MSME sector are reviewed periodically in the State Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC) meetings.
• Ministry of MSME, through its field offices, regularly organizes outreach programmes in coordination with MSME/ Industry Departments of States/UTs concerned and other stakeholders like CGTMSE, SIDBI, SLBC, Banks, MSME Associations etc., to create awareness of the Scheme.
This information was given by the Union Minister of State for MSME, Sushri Shobha Karandlaje in Lok Sabha as a written reply to a question today.

National Statistical Office Organizes Camp on Annual Survey of Incorporated Service Sector Enterprises
articles.full_content:
Service sector enterprises sensitized to provide accurate data Annual survey will help in framing economic policies: Pallavi Agrawal Srivastava
The Regional Office, Jalandhar of the National Statistical Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), Government of India, organized a camp at its regional office in Jalandhar in connection with the Annual Survey of Incorporated Service Sector Enterprises (ASISSE). Representatives of service sector enterprises registered under the GSTN database from the districts of Jalandhar and Kapurthala participated in the camp.
The camp was inaugurated by Mrs. Pallavi Agrawal Srivastava, Director (ISS) and Regional Head, National Statistical Office, Regional Office, Jalandhar. Addressing the workshop, she said that the Government of India has invited various enterprises operating in the incorporated service sector to facilitate the collection of data for the survey.
She explained that the primary objective of the camp was to create awareness among enterprises about submitting accurate information to the government, including data related to Gross Value Added (GVA), production, capital formation, and employment. She emphasized that enterprises should independently fill in their data on the designated portal based on their balance sheets, enabling proper classification and ensuring the accuracy of the information submitted.
Mrs. Srivastava further stated that the data collected through the survey would assist the government in formulating informed economic policies in the future. These policies would support the growth of businesses and contribute significantly to the country's economic development.
Also present on the occasion were Mr. Arun Kumar, Assistant Director (ISS); Mrs. Harsha Chadha, Assistant Director (ISS); and Mr. Gurpreet Singh, Assistant Director (ISS).

articles.title: GOVERNMENT HIGHLIGHTS SAFETY, PERFORMANCE AND NATIONAL BENEFITS OF ETHANOL-BLENDED PETROL
articles.full_content:
So far, no decision has been taken by the Government for increasing ethanol blending with petrol beyond 20%. Any future decision regarding higher ethanol blends will be taken only after detailed scientific and technical studies and consultations with all relevant stakeholders, including automobile manufacturers, Oil Marketing Companies and research institutions. However, E85 fuel (containing 85% ethanol and 15% petrol) has been introduced exclusively for use in Flex Fuel Vehicles (FFVs) designed and certified for such fuel (E85) and does not constitute an increase in the nationwide base ethanol blending level of petrol.

Average Blending(%)
2025-26 (Nov-June)
Ethanol is not a new fuel. It has been used globally for over a century, with countries such as Brazil operating higher ethanol blends for decades. In India, the EBP Programme has been implemented through a phased, scientifically validated and consultative process involving NITI Aayog, automobile manufacturers, Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs), Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI), Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers (SIAM), Indian Institute of Petroleum (IIP) and other technical institutions.
India has achieved 20% ethanol blending five years ahead of the original target after more than two decades of phased policy development, stakeholder consultations, scientific evaluation and capacity creation.
The scientific studies, field validation and extensive real-world operating experience have shown that E20 is a safe, cleaner and technologically superior fuel when used in accordance with the prescribed specifications and manufacturer recommendations. The Government's assessment is based not only on laboratory research but also on large-scale experience after nationwide implementation.
E15+ blended petrol has been in widespread use for over three-and-a-half years and E19-E20 fuel for over two-and-a-half years. More than 20 crore two-wheelers and over 3 crore petrol cars have been operating on these blends without any verified evidence of widespread engine failure or vehicle breakdown attributable to ethanol blending. This is further validated through large-scale real-world experience. Manufacturer service data confirm that there is no abnormal corrosion, wear or reduction in vehicle life due to E20 fuel. Manufacturers continue to honour warranty obligations for vehicles using E20 fuel, providing further confidence in its safety and reliability.
One of the country's largest passenger vehicle manufacturers serviced 2.84 crore vehicles during FY 2025–26, including approximately 1.5 crore older, non-E20-certified vehicles, without reporting any E20-related engine damage or abnormal component wear. PIB Kochi organizes Regional Media Workshop at Kasaragod
PIB Kochi, July 23, 2026
The Press Information Bureau (PIB), Kochi, under the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, organized a regional media workshop (Vartalap) for local media personnel at Kasaragod today. The workshop organized in association with the Kasaragod Press Club was inaugurated by Shri Arjun Pandian, IAS, District Collector, Kasaragod.
In his inaugural address, Shri Arjun Pandian stated that the media, as the fourth pillar of democracy, plays a crucial role in bringing the grievances of ordinary citizens to the attention of the government and facilitating their resolution. He pointed out that the media should act as a bridge connecting the concerns and feedback of the public with administrators.
Highlighting that while modern technology poses several challenges in the field of journalism, the District Collector emphasized that journalists must remain vigilant in countering fake news and ensuring the veracity of information. He added that regional media workshops like 'Vartalap' help media personnel effectively communicate various welfare schemes of the government to eligible beneficiaries.
PIB, Kerala Lakshadweep Region Additional Director General Shri V. Palanichamy, IIS, presided over the session. Addressing the gathering, Shri Palanichamy said that journalists play a pivotal role in delivering development-oriented news to the public, and such news deserves greater prominence in mainstream reporting. He highlighted the need for continuous skill upgradation among media professionals to keep pace with the rapidly evolving media landscape. Emphasizing the importance of countering misinformation, he added that accurate and responsible journalism is the need of the hour.
Padma Shri Satyanarayana Beleri, and senior journalist from Kasaragod Shri M O Varghese were felicitated as part of the event.
Ms.Dhanya Sanal K IIS, Director, PIB Kochi and All India Radio, Kochi graced the gathering. Smt. Shamila K.Y., Media and Communication Officer, PIB Kochi, delivered the welcome address. Shri Siju Kannan, President, Press Club, Kasaragod, delivered the keynote address, and Shri Pradeep Narayanan, Secretary, Press Club, Kasaragod, proposed the vote of thanks.

Manoj, Assistant Director (Staff), Office of the Postmaster General, Northern Region, Calicut. He spoke at length about the various initiatives and services offered by the Department of Posts.
Dr. A. K. Zakir Hussain, Director, Institute of Cooperative Management (ICM), Kannur, delivered a session on the Ministry of Cooperation's awareness and capacity-building initiatives. He underscored the need for cooperative banks to diversify into viable non-banking activities to ensure long-term sustainability.

NEP 2020 is laying the foundation for an inclusive, skill-centric and future-ready education system – Union Minister of State for Education
articles.full_content:
Dr Sukanta Majumdar stresses on the importance of strengthening implementation of centrally sponsored schemes for achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 Two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers concludes
Union Minister of State for Education and Development of North Eastern Region, Dr. Sukanta Majumdar, today highlighted the transformative role of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in shaping an inclusive, skill-centric and future-ready education system. He was addressing the concluding session of the two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL), Ministry of Education, at the ICAR–National Agricultural Science Complex (NASC), New Delhi.
Dr Majumdar emphasised on the need to address existing challenges through collaborative efforts between the Centre and the States and UTs to ensure that every child, teacher and school were empowered to realise their full potential. He reiterated that the effective implementation of Centrally Sponsored Schemes is essential to achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.
The Minister underscored the importance of PM POSHAN and Tithi Bhojan in improving the nutritional well-being of children, particularly those from marginalized communities. He called upon States and UTs to continue strengthening school education through coordinated efforts and sustained focus on quality, equity and inclusion.
The second day of the Conclave focused on key components of Samagra Shiksha and PM POSHAN under the PRAGATI platform. The sessions featured presentations, followed by suggestions and inputs from education ministers and officials of States and UTs. An open-house discussion was also organized on strengthening the implementation of centrally sponsored schemes, sharing best practices, and improving learning outcomes in school education.
The Conclave provided a valuable platform for the Centre and the States and UTs to share experiences, deliberate on implementation challenges and identify opportunities for further strengthening the delivery of centrally sponsored schemes. The deliberations reaffirmed the collective commitment towards ensuring equitable, inclusive and quality school education for every learner and accelerating progress towards Viksit Bharat 2047.
Presentations were made on the PM SHRI scheme by Additional Secretary, DoSEL, Shri Dheeraj Sahu; on ULLAS by Additional Secretary, DoSEL, Ms. Archana Sharma Awasthi; on inclusive education by Economic Advisor, DoSEL, Ms. A. Srija; on skill education by Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Ms. Prachi Pandey; and on PM POSHAN by Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Shri Asheesh Joshi. Joint Secretary, DoSEL, Dr. Amarpreet Duggal and other senior officials of the Union Ministry of Education, also attended the Conclave.
articles.summary: Dr Sukanta Majumdar stresses on the importance of strengthening implementation of centrally sponsored schemes for achieving the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 Two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers concludes Union Minister of State for Education and Development of North Eastern Region, Dr. Sukanta Majumdar, today highlighted the transformative role of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in shaping an inclusive, skill-centric and future-ready education system. He was addressing the concluding session of the two-day Conclave of State and UT Education Ministers to review Centrally Sponsored Schemes of the Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL), Ministry of Education, at the ICAR–National Agricultural Science Complex (NASC), New Delhi.

Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo (BRESE) 2026 to be Held from 2–5 November in New Delhi: Shri Pralhad Joshi
articles.full_content:
Summit to be held alongside the 9th Session of the International Solar Alliance (ISA) Assembly and the 4th International Conference on Green Hydrogen
The Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo (BRESE) 2026 will be held from 2nd to 5th November 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, said Shri Pralhad Joshi, Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy and Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, while addressing the Curtain Raiser of the BRESE 2026 here today.
In his keynote address, Shri Pralhad Joshi said that the BRESE 2026 will, for the first time, bring together three major renewable energy events under one platform—BRESE 2026, the 9th Session of the International Solar Alliance (ISA) Assembly, and the 4th International Conference on Green Hydrogen (ICGH). Shri Joshi further informed that the Prime Minister has agreed to inaugurate BRESE 2026. He said the Prime Minister's presence reflects India's commitment to renewable energy as a key pillar of Viksit Bharat and its contribution to global sustainable development.
Shri Joshi further stated that the Summit will bring together leading renewable energy developers, technology companies, manufacturers, battery storage firms, green hydrogen pioneers, financial institutions, international organisations, and investors, ensuring representation of the entire renewable energy value chain under one roof. He added that the Summit has also been designed with a truly global outlook. Invitations have been extended to 34 countries for the BRESE Summit, while the International Solar Alliance has invited 128 countries for the Assembly.
Shri Joshi emphasized that India’s credibility on the global stage is built on its strong track record of delivery, which has enabled it to convene the global renewable energy community. He further stated that India will soon cross the landmark of 300 GW of installed non-fossil fuel capacity. He also informed that the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana is expected to cross 50 lakh beneficiary households in the coming weeks.
Concluding his address, the Union Minister described the Bharat Renewable Energy Summit & Expo 2026 as a significant milestone in India’s engagement with the global renewable energy community. He said the Summit reflects the growth of the sector, emerging opportunities, and India’s commitment to global collaboration in advancing the clean energy transition. He extended a warm invitation to governments, industry leaders, investors, researchers, innovators, entrepreneurs, and development partners worldwide to participate in the Summit at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, from 2nd to 5th November 2026. Shri Joshi expressed confidence that the Summit will foster new partnerships, catalyse investments, promote innovation, and strengthen international cooperation in the renewable energy sector.
During the Curtain Raiser, Shri Joshi also unveiled the official logo of BRESE 2026, ‘URJHA Chakra’, and launched the official digital portal and mobile application for the Summit

Bihar Chief Minister Shri Samrat Choudhary and Union Minister Shri Jayant Chaudhary Announce Permanent Campus of NSTI (Women), Patna; MSDE and Bihar Government Exchange MoU for Skill India International Centre (SIIC) Patna
articles.full_content:
In a major boost to women’s skill development and global employment opportunities for Bihar’s youth, Chief Minister of Bihar, Shri Samrat Choudhary and Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Skill Development and Entrepreneurship and Minister of State for Education, Government of India, Shri Jayant Chaudhary, today announced the establishment of the permanent campus of the National Skill Training Institute for Women, NSTI (W), Patna and Skill India International, Patna.
The foundation stone for the permanent campus of NSTI (Women), Patna was laid at the project site by Shri Dilip Kumar, Director General, Directorate General of Training (DGT). On the occasion, the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), Government of India, and the Government of Bihar also exchanged a Memorandum of Understanding for the establishment of a Skill India International Centre (SIIC) in Bihar.
Together, the two initiatives will strengthen advanced vocational training for women and create structured pathways for Bihar’s youth to access international employment opportunities.
The permanent campus of NSTI (W), Patna, will be developed at Bihta with an investment of ₹99.97 crore. The campus will include a modern Administrative Block, Academic Block and Girls’ Hostel, enabling the institute to substantially expand its training capacity and introduce industry-aligned and future-ready courses.
The proposed Skill India International Centre, supported by a capital investment of approximately ₹7 crore from the Government of India, will prepare Bihar’s youth for global careers through foreign-language training, international employment counselling, certification guidance, pre-departure orientation and safe-migration support.
Shri Samrat Choudhary, Chief Minister said, “The foundation stone laying of the National Skill Training Institute for Women, NSTI (W), at Sikandarpur, Bihta, and the exchange of the MoU between the Government of India and the Government of Bihar for establishing the Skill India International Centre mark an important step in strengthening Bihar’s skilling ecosystem. To be developed at a cost of approximately ₹99.97 crore, the modern NSTI (W) campus will provide young women with quality, industry-aligned training and prepare them for emerging employment opportunities. The Skill India International Centre will similarly equip Bihar’s youth with internationally benchmarked skills and open new pathways to employment in India and overseas. Together, these initiatives will connect our youth with skills, self-reliance and global opportunities, while giving fresh momentum to our shared vision of a Samriddh Bihar and a Viksit Bharat.”
Speaking on the occasion, Shri Jayant Chaudhary said , “Women-led development is central to India’s growth story. The permanent campus of NSTI (W), Patna, reflects our commitment to providing women with access to modern infrastructure, industry-relevant skills and new opportunities. articles.full_content:
The MSME-Development & Facilitation Office (MSME-DFO), Gangtok, under the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India, today inaugurated a Six-Week Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programme (ESDP) on Tailoring at Gangtok.
The training programme aims to equip aspiring entrepreneurs and unemployed youth with tailoring skills and entrepreneurial competencies for self-employment and enterprise creation.
The inaugural function was held at the Programme Hall of MSME-DFO, Gangtok, in the presence of senior officials from the banking sector, State Government, and the Ministry of MSME.
The programme was inaugurated by Shri Gopal Lama, Convenor, State Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC), Sikkim, as the Chief Guest, and Shri Janak Chettri, General Manager, District Industries Centre (DIC), Gangtok, as the Guest of Honour.
Welcoming the dignitaries and participants, Shri Nirmal Chowdhury, Assistant Director and Head of Office, MSME-DFO, Gangtok, highlighted the objectives of the ESDP. He stated that the initiative aims to develop skilled entrepreneurs by combining technical training with entrepreneurship development, thereby creating sustainable livelihood opportunities and promoting self-employment among the youth of Sikkim. He also reiterated the Ministry of MSME's continued commitment to strengthening the MSME ecosystem through capacity-building programmes.
Addressing the gathering, Shri Gopal Lama, Convenor, SLBC, Sikkim, underscored the significance of skill development initiatives in enhancing employment opportunities and fostering entrepreneurship. He encouraged the trainees to make optimum use of the training programme and highlighted the crucial role of financial institutions in supporting new entrepreneurs through timely credit and financial inclusion.
Speaking on the occasion, Shri Janak Chettri, General Manager, DIC, Gangtok, emphasized the importance of close coordination between the District Industries Centre and MSME-DFO in promoting entrepreneurship across the State. He noted that collaborative efforts between Central and State Government agencies are essential for creating a vibrant ecosystem that nurtures MSMEs, facilitates enterprise growth, and generates sustainable employment opportunities in Sikkim.
During the programme, Dr. Rahul Kamble, Assistant Director, MSME-DFO, Gangtok, delivered an informative session on various flagship schemes of the Ministry of MSME. He apprised the participants about initiatives such as PM Vishwakarma, PMEGP, MSME Innovative Scheme, MSE-CDP, CGTMSE, ZED Certification, and other promotional schemes aimed at supporting micro and small enterprises through financial assistance, technology upgradation, skill development, and market access.

Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has put in place well-defined mechanisms to ensure timely collection and publication of official statistics
articles.full_content:
The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) has put in place well-defined mechanisms to ensure timely collection and publication of official statistics. Important mechanisms which are in place to ensure timely collection and publication of official statistics are outlined below.
Primary data collection for National Sample Surveys (NSS) are carried out on digital platforms such as Computer Assisted Personal Interview (CAPI) and the e-SIGMA system, incorporating in-built validation checks, real-time data monitoring, AI-enabled chatbots, and multilingual interfaces. Use of such digital platforms ensure consistency at the stage of data collection and facilitate real-time submission and validation of survey data. Further, the practice of physical scrutiny and inspection by higher-level officers is continuously undertaken as per fixed norms along with regular training for field staff to ensure the quality of the data collected. These initiatives have substantially improved data quality and led to reduction in time lag in release of survey data from 8-9 months to 45-90 days. Annual Survey Results are now released within 90-120 days; quarterly results within 45-60 days and monthly results within 15-30 days of completion of surveys.
Similarly, the price data for the Consumer Price Index (CPI) are collected through the CAPI system under the new CPI series (Base Year 2024), incorporating real-time validation checks, geo-tagging and time-stamping of observations to strengthen data quality, monitoring, transparency and timeliness. Likewise, the estimates of National Accounts Statistics (NAS) are compiled and revised in accordance with the System of National Accounts 2008 (SNA 2008), using the latest available data sourced from various administrative databases and sample surveys. Similarly, adequate mechanism are in place to ensure timely collection and publication of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP). Monthly data is collected from data sources by 21st day of every month. Regular follow-up is undertaken to improve timeliness and response rates. For the compilation of IIP, data are subjected to validation and consistency checks to ensure their quality and reliability.
Further, MoSPI enhances accountability and ensures timely dissemination of statistical products/ publications through the Advance Release Calendar (ARC), which is placed on the Ministry's website and followed for the release of official statistical publications.
To promote transparency and public accountability, anonymized unit-level survey data are made available through MoSPI’s revamped Microdata Portal, enabling researchers, policymakers, and other stakeholders to access, analyze, and utilize official statistics for evidence-based research and policy making.

articles.title: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation undertakes periodic methodological reviews of statistical framework to ensure oﬃcial statistics remain representative of current structure of economy
articles.full_content:
Modernization of National Sample Surveys has been done through various initiatives such as adoption of digital platforms such as Computer Assisted Personal Interview and e-SIGMA system
The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) undertakes periodic methodological reviews of the statistical framework to ensure that oﬃcial statistics remain representative of the current structure of the economy and continue to meet emerging data requirements. The major methodological reviews undertaken by the Ministry are outlined below.
MoSPI has undertaken comprehensive base revision exercises for the Gross Domestic Product (GDP), All India Index of Industrial Production (IIP) and Consumer Price Index (CPI) to ensure that these macroeconomic indicators adequately reflect the evolving structure of the economy and align with international statistical standards. Accordingly, the base year of GDP has been revised from 2011–12 to 2022–23 based on the recommendations of the Advisory Committee on National Accounts Statistics (ACNAS); the base year of IIP has been revised from 2011–12 to 2022–23 under the guidance of the Technical Advisory Committee on IIP; and the base year of CPI has been revised from 2012=100 to 2024=100 under the guidance of an Expert Group constituted for the purpose. These revisions incorporate updated data sources, expanded coverage, revised item baskets and weights, improved estimation methodologies, enhanced use of administrative data sources such as GST and the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) database, improved estimation of the unincorporated sector using the Annual Survey of Unincorporated Sector Enterprises (ASUSE) and the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), adoption of internationally accepted statistical practices, and other methodological refinements.
In addition, many technical consultations and brainstorming sessions were held with experts, academicians, State Governments and other stakeholders for seeking their inputs and feedback for base revision exercise of aforementioned macro economic indicators. Moreover, pre-release consultative workshops were also held before the release of the revised series of these indicators.
Further, MoSPI continuously reviews and updates the statistical standards, concepts, classiﬁcations, survey instruments and methodologies adopted in National Sample Surveys (NSS) to meet emerging data requirements, align with national and international best practices, and improve the quality, relevance and timeliness of oﬃcial statistics. Accordingly, survey schedules/questionnaires, sampling design, concepts and deﬁnitions, item classiﬁcations, ﬁeld procedures, data collection methodology, validation protocols and tabulation plans are reviewed before each major survey based on recommendations of the Steering Committee for National Sample Surveys (NSS), Technical Advisory Groups (TAGs), survey-speciﬁc Technical Expert Groups(TEGs), and consultations with Central Ministries/Departments, State Go

Review of PMKVY
articles.full_content:
The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), launched in 2015, has progressively evolved towards demand-driven, industry-aligned and outcome-oriented skilling. PMKVY 1.0 established the national framework for short-term training, certification and Recognition of Prior Learning; PMKVY 2.0 expanded sectoral and geographical coverage, introduced State-led implementation and promoted convergence with national priorities and missions; while PMKVY 3.0 strengthened decentralized, bottom-up planning through State Skill Development Missions and District Skill Committees for identifying local skill gaps, employment opportunities and training requirements. Under PMKVY 4.0, training targets and job roles are prioritized based on national priorities and missions, State and District Skill Development Plans, industry demand, skill-gap assessments, institutional capacity and implementation readiness. The Scheme also prioritizes new-age and futuristic job roles in areas such as Artificial Intelligence, cyber security, semiconductors, 5G, Internet of Things, drone technology, green energy and additive manufacturing, thereby linking national development priorities and regional workforce requirements with employment oriented skills.
Further, Skill Impact Bond, launched through the National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), was designed to benefit 50,000 youth over four years, with at least 60 per cent women, by shifting the focus from training and certification alone to sustained employment outcomes. As on 1 st July, 2026, 51,869 candidates have been enrolled and 48,437 have been certified under this initiative.
The Ministry and the NSDC have exchanged anMemorandum of Understanding (MoU) for establishing a Skills Outcomes Fund on 15 th July, 2026 with a proposed corpus of approximately ₹530 crore. Anchored by NSDC under the aegis of MSDE, the Fund seeks to mobilize Government support, Corporate Social Responsibility investments and philanthropic capital to support over two lakh youth through industry-aligned skilling, employment, entrepreneurship and sustainable livelihood pathways. The Fund also focuses onplatform for piloting innovative approaches, including skill vouchers, skills-financing mechanisms and entrepreneurship-led models, with greater accountability for measurable outcomes.
These reforms demonstrate the Ministry’s continuing shift from an input- and training-volume-based approach to a demand-responsive framework focused on measurable employment, retention, income and livelihood outcomes.
The Ministry conducts periodic independent evaluations of PMKVY to ensure quality and accountability. An evaluation by NITI Aayog (October 2020) indicated that approximately 94 percent of surveyed employers expressed willingness to hire more candidates trained under PMKVY. The study also noted that 52 percent of candidates placed in employment or oriented under the Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) component reported receiving higher salaries or expecting imp
articles.summary: The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), launched in 2015, has progressively evolved towards demand-driven, industry-aligned and outcome-oriented skilling. PMKVY 1.0 established the national framework for short-term training, certification and Recognition of Prior Learning; PMKVY 2.0 expanded sectoral and geographical coverage, introduced State-led implementation and promoted convergence with national priorities and missions; while PMKVY 3.0 strengthened decentralized, bottom-up planning through State Skill Development Missions and District Skill Committees for identifying local skill gaps, employment opportunities and training requirements. Under PMKVY 4.0, training targets and job roles are prioritized based on national priorities and missions, State and District Skill Development Plans, industry demand, skill-gap assessments, institutional capacity and implementation readiness.

Progress of PMKVY
articles.full_content:
Under the Government of India’s Skill India Mission (SIM), the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) delivers skill, re-skill and up-skill training through an extensive network of skill development centres under various schemes, viz. Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS), National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) and Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS) through Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), to all the sections of the society across the country. The SIM aims at enabling the youth of India to get future-ready, equipped with industry-relevant skills.
In order to expand the skill development ecosystem in the country, ensure active engagement of the industry and to expand access to the skill training, MSDE has inter-alia taken the following steps:
• National Council for Vocational Education and Training (NCVET) has been set up as an overarching regulator establishing regulations and standards to ensure quality in the Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) space. NCVET recognises the industry bodies as the Awarding Bodies (ABs) and/or Assessment Agencies (AAs).
• The Awarding Bodies are expected to develop the qualifications as per the industry demand and map them with the identified occupations as per the National Classification of Occupations, 2015 and obtain industry validations. NCVET has approved 10209 qualifications as per the industry requirements, out of which 2975 qualifications are valid and active, and 7234 qualifications are archived.
• Under various schemes of MSDE, the training centres with requisite quality have been set-up or engaged for delivering the short-term and long term training. 13633 training centres have been set-up or engaged under PMKVY up to 30.06.2026 and 294 training centres have been set up under JSS Scheme. Also, there are 13856 ITIs and 33 NSTIs under the aegis of Directorate General of Training (DGT) spread across the country.
• Also, to enhance the overall quality and relevance of vocational training in the country, Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs (PM SETU) scheme has been launched. The scheme inter-alia aims to upgrade 1,000 Government ITIs (200 Hub ITIs and 800 Spoke ITIs) in a Hub and Spoke model, wherein upgradation includes smart classrooms, modern labs, digital content, and new courses aligned to industry needs. It also aims to augment capacity of five NSTIs located in Bhubaneswar, Chennai, Hyderabad, Kanpur, and Ludhiana, including setting-up sector-specific National Centres of Excellence for skilling, with focus on advanced training of trainers with global partnership.
• DGT is implementing Flexi MoU Scheme and Dual System of Training (DST) in collaboration with industries to provide training to ITI students in real industrial environment.
• DGT has signed MoU with IT Tech and other companies like IBM, Microsoft, Autodesk, AWS, Shell, etc. to ensure industry linkages for the institut

Shri Ramchandra Kisan Jayabhaye Assumes Charge as Chief Postmaster General, Maharashtra Circle
articles.full_content:
Mumbai, 22 July 2026
Shri Ramchandra Kisan Jayabhaye has assumed charge as the Chief Postmaster General (CPMG), Maharashtra Circle, with effect from 9 July 2026.
An officer of the Indian Postal Service (IPoS), 2000 batch, Shri Jayabhaye brings over 25 years of experience spanning postal administration, banking, operations and institutional management.
Shri Jayabhaye has held several key assignments and contributed to the modernization and expansion of postal services across the country. He played an important role in the implementation of Project Arrow, aimed at upgrading and modernizing post offices in Aurangabad and Nagpur. As Director of Freighter Operations, he also oversaw and streamlined cargo operations involving four dedicated mail aircraft.
In the Chhattisgarh Circle, Shri Jayabhaye contributed significantly to expanding the postal network in Left Wing Extremism (LWE) affected areas, facilitating the opening of 391 Branch Post Offices. He also spearheaded collaboration with the Regional Transport Office (RTO) for the doorstep delivery of official documents through the postal network.
Shri Jayabhaye, prior to assuming charge as Chief Postmaster General, Maharashtra Circle, served as General Manager (Operations) at the Postal Life Insurance (PLI) Directorate, New Delhi, where he centrally steered the development of the core Insurance Management System (IMS) software to modernize the operations of Postal Life Insurance and Rural Postal Life Insurance.
With extensive experience in technology-driven modernization, financial inclusion and postal administration, Shri Jayabhaye's appointment is expected to further strengthen the delivery of postal and citizen-centric services across the Maharashtra Circle.
PIB Mumbai | Robin Singh/Darshana Rane

An officer of the Indian Postal Service (IPoS), 2000 batch, Shri Jayabhaye brings over 25 years of experience spanning postal administration, banking, operations and institutional management. Shri Jayabhaye has held several key assignments and contributed to the modernization and expansion of postal services across the country.

articles.title: Smt. R. Revathi, District Collector, Thanjavur inaugurated New Aadhar Seva Kendra
articles.full_content:
Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) operationalized a new state -of-the art Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) at Thanjavur , on Wednesday, 22 nd July 2026 marking another step towards strengthening resident - centric Aadhaar services in the city of Thanjavur.
The ASK Thanjavur was inaugurated by Tmt. R. Revathi, I AS , District Collector Thanjavur as a Chief Guest, in the presence of Officials from UIDAI RO Bengaluru and others.
She appreciated the UIDAI’s effort and interacted with residents who visited ASK as well as the centre staff present there. Also, enquired about the Aadhaar enrolment and update procedures, and reviewed the various Aadhaar services being provided at the centre.
The ASK Thanjavur is equipped with 8 operational kits to provide seamless Aadhaar -related services to thousands of residents. The ASK is functional on all days except public holidays, thereby ensuring greater accessibility & convenience. The Aadhaar Seva Kendra offers Aadhaar enrolment, demographic and biometric updates, grievance redressal and other Aadhaar related services under one roof.
In addition to the enrolment and update transactions, ASK Thanjavur will also receive grievances from Aadhaar number holders for their resolution through the Regional Office.
With the inauguration of the Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) at Thanjavur , UIDAI has now operationalised 10 Aadhaar Seva Kendras across Tamil nadu . A further 20 ASKs are scheduled to become operational in the coming days across various districts, increasing the total 30 UIDAI -run Aadhaar Seva Kendras in the State.
The ASKs run by UIDAI, are designed to be fully inclusive and resident -friendly with facilities such as wheelchair accessibility, accessible restrooms, and a dedicated grievance redressal mechanism. Residents can also book appointments online for Aadhaar s ervices at ASKs through UIDAI’s appointment portal: https://bookappointment.uidai.gov.in/ .
M/s BLS International will be managing the daily operations of this ASK.

Revathi, I AS , District Collector Thanjavur as a Chief Guest, in the presence of Officials from UIDAI RO Bengaluru and others. She appreciated the UIDAI’s effort and interacted with residents who visited ASK as well as the centre staff present there.
